summary: Replaced progress.yaml writes with append-only summary.jsonl, updated summary loading in runner/app paths, and added JSONL regression coverage. Verification passed: go test ./..., go build ./..., strict OpenSpec validate, diff check, and temp jq acceptance.

Added commitSetupFiles helper to cmd/rally/main.go. After rally init, stages .rally/.gitignore, .rally/config.toml, .rally/README.md and commits as 'rally: initialize workspace'. After laps.InstallHooks, stages .laps/hooks.json and hook scripts and commits as 'rally: install laps hooks'. Both use --no-verify, skip when nothing staged, never sweep user changes, and warn gracefully on git failures. go build and go test pass. Smoke-tested: first init creates exactly one setup commit, second is no-op, dirty working tree files are not swept.

Added 5 tests for commitSetupFiles in cmd/rally/main_test.go covering: init creates exactly one 'rally: initialize workspace' commit, init rerun is no-op, dirty working tree user files are not swept, hook install creates exactly one 'rally: install laps hooks' commit, and hook reinstall is no-op. All use real git repos via gitx helpers. Concurrent-rally duplicate-commit coverage is best-effort — the sequential tests prove idempotency for single-process flows; a true concurrent race would need goroutine-based stress testing with serialization points that are inherently non-deterministic.

Added lap-boundary commit instructions to both hook scripts. laps-done-hook.sh now instructs agent to commit with '<lap-description>: done'. laps-handoff-hook.sh now instructs agent to commit with '<lap-description>: in progress (handoff)'. Both include guidance to replace the placeholder. Added assertions in hooks_test.go for commit message forms and placeholder guidance. All go test ./internal/laps/... tests pass.
